column1: U.S. Food Purveyor & Ordering Portal Registry

column2: A working integration registry of U.S. foodservice purveyors, public websites, customer ordering URLs, and public order-template/import documentation.

There is no single authoritative public list of every U.S. purveyor. IFDA reports roughly 17,100 U.S. foodservice-distribution facilities, while public association directories cover only subsets of companies and locations.
141 companies/networks: U.S.-based IFDA entries plus selected national, online, produce and specialty purveyors.
A direct portal is included only when publicly verifiable. Otherwise the field is blank and Portal Status explains the limitation.
Official vendor templates are preferred. Third-party CSV/export instructions are clearly labeled; many vendor templates are only downloadable after login.
Seed a platformVendorRegistry, then add aliases/domains and let customers submit missing regional purveyors for admin approval.
Systematically enumerate UniPro, Frosty Acres, Golbon and state/regional directories; verify portal technology, CSV schema and upload capability account-by-account.
Choose a two-letter customer state code in E13, then filter “Match Selected State?” to MATCH on the Geographic Coverage sheet.
Only explicitly confirmed state territories auto-match. National and online-nationwide vendors match every state; vague regional claims do not.
A state match is a candidate, not a delivery guarantee. Many purveyors serve only selected counties, metros or routes within a state.
High = explicit official territory or national footprint; Medium = official facilities/regions with partial inference; Low = preliminary regional inference needing verification.
Buying groups, redistributors and franchise supply systems remain in the registry but are intentionally not treated as direct local vendor matches.
Every row includes a public source URL. Use the exact customer ZIP or vendor sales contact for final qualification.

column1: FnB Cost Pro Vendor Master v2.0

column2: Production-oriented seed master for FnB Cost Pro's platformVendorRegistry and vendor discovery workflow.
Vendor Master v2 contains canonical names, aliases, countries, geography, ordering portals, connector keys, ownership status and seed decisions.
A normalized, database-friendly subset designed for direct import or transformation in Replit.
Sufficiently verified direct vendors and online purveyors suitable for initial seeding.
Real distributors with incomplete website, territory, alias or current-status verification. Queue these for global_admin review.
Buying groups, redistributors, contract systems and closed franchise supply networks that should not normally appear as a restaurant's direct vendor.
Includes the current international IFDA membership found in Canada, Bermuda, Brazil and South Korea, plus U.S. distributors with cross-border reach.
Parent ownership does not automatically replace an operating vendor brand. Cheney remains a vendor identity under PFG; Restaurant Depot remains separate while its Sysco transaction is pending.
State/province matches are candidates only. Final availability still requires customer ZIP/postal-code confirmation.
